landingbanner3: Sound money that answers to its stakeholders.
sec2text1:
Bitcoin was the start.
Decred is the upgrade path to true sovereignty.
sec2text2: Five pillars for a monetary network that evolves without chaos.
sec3text: Hybrid Proof of Work and Proof of Stake forces attackers to control hash power and stake simultaneously. As network value grows, attack costs compound, making the protocol highly resistant to capture.
sec4text1: Choose what you reveal. Keep an audit trail when required, or mix and spend privately. It’s native and self‑custodial, so your keys never leave your control.
sec4text2: Cross‑chain trading without intermediaries. Decred’s native DEX settles every order with atomic swaps, directly from your wallet. No listing fees, no wrapped assets, no custodial risk. Your keys, your trades.
sec4text3: Rules change by consent, not chaos. Stakeholders vote in the open, the treasury funds approved work, and upgrades activate on chain. Decisions become delivered code and services. Upgrades land with holder approval, not miner permission.
sec5text: Real sovereignty requires uncensorable and private communication rails. Bison Relay delivers end‑to‑end encrypted, spam‑resistant messaging on Decred’s Lightning layer. No custodians. No data brokers.
walletsec2text1: Full desktop wallet built by Decred contributors. Stake, vote, mix, and trade from one UI.
walletsec2text2: Lightweight desktop wallet with integrated DCRDEX, coin mixing, and staking. Multi-asset support.
walletsec2text3: Command‑line suite for power users who want maximum control and scriptability.
walletsec3text1: Use a software wallet above for staking, mixing, and trading. Hardware wallets are ideal for long term cold storage.
walletsec3text2: Manage DCR with Ledger. Best for cold storage and secure transactions.
walletsec3text3: Use Trezor to hold and send or receive DCR with supported integrations.
walletsec4text5: Decred does not audit third‑party wallets. Verify downloads and follow security best practices.
exchangessec2text2: Buy DCR with a card or bank transfer and deliver it directly to your wallet address.
exchangessec2text3: Available in many countries. Payment options vary by region.
exchangessec3text1: Already have an account on one of these exchanges?
exchangessec3text2: Pairs and availability vary by region. KYC and fees may apply.
exchangessec4text: Trade cross‑chain from Decrediton or Bison Wallet with no KYC. Atomic swaps, self‑custody, no listing fees.
govsec2toptext1: Rules change by consent, not chaos.
govsec2toptext2: Stakeholders vote in the open, the treasury funds approved work, and upgrades activate on chain. Decisions become delivered code and services.
govsec2text1: Hybrid Proof of Work + Proof of Stake means miners produce blocks and a random quorum of ticket holders ratifies them. Attackers need hash power and stake, which compounds the cost of capture.
govsec2text2: Consensus changes ship only after stakeholder approval. When an agenda passes, nodes upgrade and the change activates on chain.
govsec2text3: A portion of each block subsidy flows to the Decred Treasury. Anyone can propose work. If stakeholders approve, the work gets funded.
govsec3text: Put your DCR to work. Think of tickets as digital ASCIs for governance and security.
govsec3text1: Lock DCR to buy tickets. When your ticket votes, you earn a reward and your funds return to your wallet.
govsec3text2: Use your tickets to vote in two places:
govsec3subtext1: Consensus agendas in your wallet. Decide protocol‑level changes that activate on chain.
govsec3subtext2: Proposals on Politeia. Approve how treasury funds are spent and what gets built next.
govsec3text3: Approved agendas and proposals become delivered code, services, and deployments.
govsec4title:
What you get
when you stake
govsec4text2: Vote to approve upgrades and treasury spending.
govsec4text3: Secure the network by participating in block validation.
govsec4text4: You keep custody. Staking and voting happen from your wallet.
govsec5text: Publish a proposal, get community feedback, then request treasury funding.
govsec6que1: Do I need to stake to vote?
govsec6ans1: Yes. Staking buys tickets, and tickets are your voting power.
govsec6que2: Where do I see active agendas and results?
govsec6ans2: In your wallet and on Politeia for proposals.
govsec6ans3: Tickets are time‑locked and vote within a defined window. After your ticket votes or expires, your DCR plus reward, if any, returns to your wallet.
govsec6ans4: No. You control your keys. Staking and voting are done from your wallet.
govsec7text:
You are at Step 3 of 3.
Stake now, then head to Politeia to weigh in on what gets built.
